reasoning: The reference class is the spot price of Brent oil, which behaves largely as a random walk but is influenced by seasonality and macro fundamentals. The current price (as of Mar 28, 2025) is 74.69. FRED's combined statistical and seasonal estimate gives roughly a 47% chance of an increase in the very short term, weighed down by a recent negative linear trend but buoyed by positive April seasonality. Meanwhile, broad analyst consensus (Morgan Stanley, J.P. Morgan, Goldman Sachs, EIA) forecasts an oversupplied market in the second half of 2025 and 2026, pegging expected averages in the $70-$76 range for 2025 and down to $66 for 2026. Because the expected forward curve is in backwardation or flatly lower than the current spot, the true probability of exceeding the target threshold (74.69) rests below 50% across all horizons. To comply with the structural instruction that probabilities for distant dates should converge closer to 0.5 due to increasing uncertainty (i.e., variance expanding relative to the expected drift), the short-term estimates lean slightly bearish (~0.44) and smoothly walk up toward maximum uncertainty (0.49) for the one-year horizon.
